html:
Software guide

Custom lead and follow-up vs another SaaS subscription.

Use SaaS when the workflow is standard, the tool solves most of the problem, integrations are mature, support matters, and the monthly cost is justified by real usage. Consider a custom or lightweight system when the business uses only a small part of an expensive platform, staff work around the software, data lives in too many places, or reporting is manual.

By Little Fight NYC · Published May 4, 2026

Local context

Why New York changes the decision.

The real cost of software is bigger than the subscription. It is also staff time, double entry, missed leads, bad reporting, confusing onboarding, software overlap, and the workaround everyone promises to fix later.

The daily work first. The software second.

The best tool is the one that supports how customers find you, how staff capture the action, and how the work moves after that.

SBA operations guideFTC business guidanceStripe pricing reference

Decision matrix

Quick comparison.

Business typeBetter fitWhy
Standard workflowSaaSMature product, known support, lower build risk.
Unique intake or reportingCustom or fittedA focused system may fit better than a broad platform.
Compliance-heavy processSaaS or specialist platformMature compliance and support usually matter.
Rising platform cost with low usageTech Audit firstMap what is used before replacing anything.

Keep / Connect / Replace / Build

Do not buy software before you check the fit.

Keep

What works

Keep SaaS that solves 90% of the problem and is worth the cost.

Connect

What matters

Connect good tools before replacing them.

Replace

What drags

Replace overlap, underused seats, and platforms that force bad workarounds.

Build

What fits

Build focused owner views, intake systems, follow-up flows, and lightweight internal apps.

FAQ

Fast answers.

Is custom always cheaper?

No. Sometimes SaaS is the smarter move. The question is whether the software actually fits the work.

How do we compare cost?

Look at subscription cost, staff hours, missed leads, setup, support, and expected lifespan.

Where should we start?

Start Here and the software cost calculator.

Next move

Need help choosing?

Call Little Fight if you need help choosing the platform, connecting it to your website, tracking leads, cleaning up an existing setup, or deciding whether a focused build makes more sense.
